title: Cerasus pallida
intro: Cerasus pallida, also known as the sweet cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Cerasus pallida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cerasus pallida include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Cerasus pallida belongs to the family Rosaceae and is part of the subgenus Cerasus. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China. Cerasus pallida is closely related to other species in the Cerasus subgenus, such as Cerasus jamasakura and Cerasus campanulata. The classification of Cerasus pallida is further divided into several species and cultivars, which can be found in various parts of Asia. This shrub or small tree is often cultivated for its beautiful flowers and edible fruits.

properties: Cerasus pallida, also known as the dwarf cherry, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various skin conditions. The plant's extracts are believed to possess antioxidant and antiseptic properties, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing and skin infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Cerasus pallida in treating various health conditions. However, its potential therapeutic properties have been a subject of interest in the field of ethnopharmacology.
constituents: Cerasus pallida, also known as the yellow cherry or wild cherry, contains a variety of active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, Cerasus pallida contains glycosides, including amygdalin and prunasin, which have been shown to have potential anti-cancer properties. The plant also contains coumarins, such as scopoletin and scopoletin-7-O-glucoside, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Furthermore, Cerasus pallida contains other bioactive compounds, including phenolic acids, terpenoids, and volatile oils.
parts: Cerasus pallida is a species of cherry tree, and its medicinal properties are primarily associated with its fruit, leaves, and bark. The fruit of Cerasus pallida contains anthocyanins, which are powerful antioxidants that have been linked to various health benefits. The leaves and bark of the tree are also used in traditional medicine, although their specific uses and effects are not as well-documented as those of the fruit. The plant's medicinal properties are thought to include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cerasus pallida for medicinal purposes.
preparations: Cerasus pallida, also known as the weeping cherry, is a lesser-known species in the Prunus genus. However, information on herbal preparations of this specific plant is scarce. There is limited research available on the potential medicinal uses of Cerasus pallida, and it is not commonly used in herbal medicine.
